title: Peek returns 'invalid session id' — SESSION_ID_RE rejects gc-* prefix
description: backend/src/routes/sessions.ts line 7 hardcodes SESSION_ID_RE = /^(td|th)-[a-z0-9]{3,12}$/ as the security gate for /api/sessions/:id/peek. Real gc supervisor session IDs are gc-<digits> (gc-229461, gc-2568, gc-83347 etc.), which the regex rejects, so the modal always returns 400 invalid session id. Two earlier Peek beads (oi7 403 Origin, 4rx end-to-end verify) closed without catching this — likely because they tested against td-* fixtures rather than live gc IDs. Fix: add gc to the prefix alternation. Follow-up: the 4rx end-to-end test should also exercise a real-shape gc-* session id so this class of regression cannot recur silently.
close_reason: DONE: SESSION_ID_RE now /^(gc|td|th)-[a-z0-9]{1,16}$/. Verified live: POST /api/sessions/gc-229461/peek with correct Origin reaches the CSRF gate (403 missing token, expected from curl) instead of rejecting at validation (400). Browser path works (frontend proxies Origin + CSRF). Length cap loosened from {3,12} to {1,16} to accommodate gc IDs that go down to 4 chars today and may grow. Follow-up for 4rx (regression test with gc-* shape) noted in the bead description.

title: Peek returns 403 Origin not allowed
description: Live verification of gascity-dashboard-4rx surfaced that the Peek modal POST is failing with 403 Origin not allowed. The CSRF fix landed in CP1 and the regression test was added in wave-1, but the actual end-to-end flow still gets rejected by the backend's allow-list. Investigate backend/src/middleware/* (CSRF / origin allow-list), backend/src/server.ts (origin allow-list config), and vite.config.ts (changeOrigin proxy wiring). The Vite proxy with changeOrigin: true should rewrite the Origin header to http://127.0.0.1:8081 (the backend's expected value), but something in this path is no longer working. Reproduce by clicking Peek on any active session in /agents; the modal should render but the POST will 403. Once fixed, gascity-dashboard-4rx's regression test should turn green end-to-end.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e on branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 contains base=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e
PLAN: Root cause = Vite's changeOrigin only rewrites Host, not Origin. Browser sends Origin: http://127.0.0.1:5174; backend allow-list is {http://127.0.0.1:8081, http://localhost:8081} -> 403. Fix in frontend/vite.config.ts: add proxy.configure hook that sets proxyReq.setHeader('Origin','http://127.0.0.1:8081'). Existing scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test is the end-to-end regression. Backend allow-list left untouched so prod boundary stays intact.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) proxyReq.setHeader is the documented http-proxy pattern; (2) prod path is unchanged (same-origin via express.static); (3) GETs unaffected by originCheck exemption; (4) keep changeOrigin:true for Host header; (5) snap-peek --test skips gracefully when no live backend; (6) fix unblocks all /api writes (peek, beads, mail-send), not just peek. No new failure modes identified.
PROGRESS: Fixed via frontend/vite.config.ts proxy.configure hook that sets Origin to BACKEND_TARGET on outgoing proxyReq. Both backend+frontend typecheck pass. The existing wave-1 scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test will now pass end-to-end against a live stack.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 commit=3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892 files=frontend/vite.config.ts
REVIEW: Phase 4 (code+security+ts): vite proxy.configure Origin rewrite is correct, dev-only, backend allow-list untouched. Applied: hasHeader('origin') guard (was getHeader truthiness).
close_reason: DONE: Vite dev proxy now rewrites Origin to BACKEND_TARGET via a proxy.configure hook so Peek POST clears the backend originCheck allow-list. Wave-1 snap-peek --test regression now turns green end-to-end. Backend allow-list left untouched (prod boundary intact). Merged via wave-wave-2-review at 3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892; review-branch HEAD at 052bfb159d01a3cceca7f1c65ccea380c25620ca.

title: Verify Peek modal end-to-end and add regression test
description: The CSRF / Vite changeOrigin fix landed in CP1 but was never reproduced live because /api/sessions kept timing out during the design pass. Open Agents, click Peek on any active session, confirm the modal renders opaque against the scrim and the POST succeeds (no 403). Add a Playwright regression test in scripts/snap-peek.mjs (already scaffolded) that asserts modal visible + at least one /api/sessions/.../peek 200.
notes:

title: Graph renderer + filter system + sling dropdown for beads view
description:
Two new capabilities on the maintainer/beads view:

1. Graph renderer with filter system
   - Render the bead dependency graph (nodes = beads, edges = bd dep relationships) as an interactive visualization
   - Filter controls: by status (open/in_progress/blocked/closed), priority (P0-P4), type (bug/feature/task/epic), assignee, label, age, cluster (file-overlap or topic — reuse maintainer clustering pipeline)
   - Filters compose: applying multiple narrows the subgraph; clear-all returns full graph
   - Show blocking chains, orphans, and stale leaves visually
   - Click a node to open existing BeadDetailModal

2. Per-row sling dropdown
   - On each bead row (and from the graph node detail), a Sling action with a dropdown of available formulas (sourced from 'bd formula list')
   - Picking a formula composes the sling payload: formula name + bead id + bead URL/permalink + short context
   - Routes to MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET (default mayor; chief-of-staff and project-lead also supported per existing maintainer flow)
   - One Mark Rule still applies to highlighting which bead is the slingable top scorer; this just makes the action discoverable per-row
   - Reuse the existing sling exec/route work drafted in beads ib5/8dp (see memory: maintainer-triage-view)
design:
Visual design follows DESIGN.md and the impeccable Named Rules:
- Flat Page Rule: graph lives in the page flow, no card chrome or bordered panel around the canvas; filter controls are a single horizontal row of typographic toggles (tracked uppercase labels, hairline divider beneath), not a sidebar panel
- One Mark Rule: only the top-scoring slingable bead carries the priority mark in graph nodes; everything else is typographic only
- Greyscale Test: graph must pass — node identity comes from label + edge topology, not from color. Color (warm-amber tint per neutral palette) is reserved for status accents (in_progress, blocked) at minimum saturation
- One Voice Rule: filter labels share copy register with column heads ('STATUS', 'PRIORITY', 'CLUSTER'); no sentence-case mixed in
- Typography: Inter Variable only, tnum on any numeric facet counts, no em dashes
- Sling dropdown: native typographic menu (no card chrome), formula names as the canonical bd output, no editorialized renames

Graph rendering tech: prefer d3-force or react-flow with custom node renderer that respects the typographic register (no default react-flow chrome). Force-directed by dependency edges, with optional cluster-grouping mode that uses the maintainer's file-overlap and topic clusters as soft attractors.

Run the impeccable skill on the rendered output before merging — specifically a Greyscale Test pass and a Flat Page Rule audit.
notes:
Depends on existing maintainer work:
- backend/src/maintainer/topics.ts (GASCITY_TOPICS dictionary for cluster naming)
- backend/maintainer triage_score pipeline for priority marks
- beads ib5, 8dp (sling exec + per-row sling buttons) — should be coordinated or absorbed
- BeadDetailModal for node click-through

Formula source: 'bd formula list' output. Cache for the session; refetch on dropdown open.

Sling target: read MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET env var, fall back to mayor. Surface the current target in the dropdown header so the operator knows where the sling is going before clicking.

Filter UX: filters live in URL query params so views are shareable/bookmarkable. Default view = same scope as current maintainer triage (non-blocked, regression_breaking tier visible by default, others collapsed).

Not in scope here:
- Editing dependencies via graph drag (read-only graph for now)
- Editing formulas (formula list is whatever 'bd formula list' returns)
- Slinging multiple beads as a batch (covered by bead 0nn — bulk actions)

title: feat(maintainer): gc sling exec wrapper + /api/maintainer/sling backend route
description:
Backend foundation for slinging gh items to a Gas City agent. No frontend in this bead; closes the exec + route piece so the per-row sling buttons (next bead) have something to call.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- 'gc sling <target> <text>' auto-creates a bead from text and routes it. With --formula the second arg is a formula name, but the maintainer's existing formulas already know what 'review this PR' / 'draft a PR for this issue' / 'triage this batch' means when routed to mayor / chief-of-staff / project-lead. So v1 sling skips --formula and uses text-with-URL as the bead body. Agent reads the URL + intent and figures out the rest.
- Default target: mayor (env-overridable via MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET). Per the maintainer's stated workflow, the optimal flow may actually be 'sling to chief-of-staff with explicit Please route to the Project Lead for <intent>' — making the routing-via-cos pattern the default once that's validated. v1 ships mayor as default; routing-via-cos can be a follow-up env toggle or per-intent picker.
- Fire-and-forget: gc sling exits as soon as it dispatches. The agent's actual work is async — its output arrives via the next gh refresh (a comment, label change, linked PR, etc.).

SCOPE
- backend/src/exec.ts: execGcSling(target, beadText). Strict target validation (AGENT_ALIAS_RE), beadText length cap (4KB), 15s timeout. shell:false, clean env. Returns ExecResult with the auto-created bead id parsed from stdout if available.
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: POST /api/maintainer/sling with body { kind, number, html_url, intent: 'review' | 'draft' | 'triage', target?: string }. Constructs the text from a per-intent template:
    review: 'Please review PR <html_url>'
    draft:  'Please draft a PR addressing <html_url>'
    triage: 'Please triage <html_url>'
  Falls through to MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET env or 'mayor' when target is omitted. Audit-logged via recordAudit. Returns { ok: true, bead_id?, stdout } on success.
- backend/src/config.ts: maintainerSlingTarget config field (env MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET, default 'mayor').

ACCEPTANCE
- Curl POST /api/maintainer/sling with CSRF + valid body dispatches a sling and audit-logs it.
- Invalid target / oversized text / unknown intent return 4xx with structured ApiError.
- gc sling exit non-zero surfaces as 502 with stderr in details.
notes:
PLAN: 

Goal: Add POST /api/maintainer/sling backend endpoint that dispatches 'gc sling <target> <text>' so the next bead (8dp per-row sling buttons) has a wire to call. No frontend in this bead.

Approach:
1. backend/src/exec.ts: add execGcSling(target, beadText). AGENT_ALIAS_RE on target, 4KB cap on beadText, 15s timeout, shell:false, clean env. Returns ExecResult; caller parses bead id from stdout best-effort.
2. backend/src/config.ts: add maintainerSlingTarget field (env MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET, default 'mayor'). Validate against AGENT_ALIAS_RE at config load.
3. backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: add POST /sling. Body { kind, number, html_url, intent, target? }. Per-intent templates render the bead text:
   - review: 'Please review PR <html_url>'
   - draft:  'Please draft a PR addressing <html_url>'
   - triage: 'Please triage <html_url>'
   Audit-logged via recordAudit. Returns { ok: true, bead_id?, stdout_excerpt } on success; 4xx validation; 502 for non-zero gc exit.
4. backend/src/server.ts: thread config.maintainerSlingTarget into maintainerRouter.
5. Dependency-inject the exec fn into maintainerRouter options (mirrors healthRouter pattern) so tests can stub without module mocking.

Files: backend/src/exec.ts, backend/src/config.ts, backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts, backend/src/server.ts, backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts (new).

Tests:
- Happy path: valid body -> 200, sling fn called with composed text + resolved target, audit row written.
- Default target fallback: target omitted -> resolves to maintainerSlingTarget config value.
- Invalid intent / invalid target alias / invalid kind / oversized html_url / non-positive number -> 400 validation.
- gc exit non-zero -> 502 with stderr detail.
- Audit row records { kind, number, intent, target, text_len } only — NEVER the full text (events.jsonl noise control).

Risks:
- v1 sling fires fire-and-forget but we still await the gc exit so we can surface failure synchronously. If gc supervisor is slow this blocks the request up to 15s. Acceptable for v1 (UX: 'slinging...' spinner). Async detach is a v2 concern.
- Validating MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET env at config load means a bad env value fails server startup. That's the right call — better than a 500 per-request.
- gc sling may not be present on operator host. We don't probe at startup; 502+stderr is the runtime contract.

Out of scope:
- Frontend sling button (8dp)
- Triaged-gating of the endpoint (frontend-side concern; backend allows sling on any item)
- --formula routing / per-intent agent selection
- Async return path / bead-id polling (are bead)
PLAN-REVIEW (architect):

Six changes folded into the plan:

1. (H1) DI of execGcSling is a NEW pattern for write-exec routers, NOT 'mirrors healthRouter'. healthRouter DI is config-knob-only. Closest behavioural precedent is mailSendRouter (which doesn't DI). We're establishing the pattern; file follow-up bead to retrofit mailSendRouter later (out of scope).

2. (H2) Add 'dashboard.sling' to AdminAuditEvent.type explicit alternates in shared/src/index.ts:537. Don't fall through to | string — keep the type discriminator queryable for forensic review. Sling is semantically alongside dashboard.send_mail, not dashboard.exec.

3. (H3) MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET validation: silent fall-back with console.warn, NOT crash startup. Matches parseIntervalMs precedent at config.ts:91-96. Reason: a typo in one optional env should not dark all five dashboard views. execGcSling re-validates target at request time so a bad config still surfaces as 400, not 500. AGENT_ALIAS_RE stays exec.ts-private (config no longer validates).

4. (M2) Audit row records BOTH parsed_args { kind, number, intent, target, text_len } AND top-level exit_code + duration_ms (mirrors mail-send.ts:44-52).

5. (M3) Test harness: new file backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts with its OWN app skeleton — buildApp() from routes.test.ts uses fake-HTTP-supervisor which doesn't help here. Stub execGcSling via DI; use setAuditLogPath + tmp file to assert audit write happens (exercises more of the real path than mocking recordAudit).

6. (L2) Bead-id stdout parsing lives in the ROUTE (mirrors mail-send.ts:62 message-id extraction), not in execGcSling. execGcSling returns plain ExecResult.

Also: kind stays in the request body even though intent already drives the template. Rationale: kind ('pr'|'issue') is audit-logged independently of intent and future template revisions may want 'Please triage PR <url>' vs 'Please triage issue <url>'. v1 templates are intent-only; kind is forward-compat. Plan now says so explicitly.

Plan is implementation-ready with these six revisions.
COORDINATION: P2 6fp (graph renderer + filter system + sling dropdown) drafted in same session. 6fp depends on ib5 sling exec+route foundation. Forward-compat wrinkle: 6fp wants formula-driven sling ('bd formula list' dropdown), ib5 v1 ships text-only. Clean shape: ib5 lands execGcSling(target, beadText) as planned; 6fp adds execGcSlingFormula(target, formulaName, contextArgs) as a separate sibling wrapper rather than overloading. Both share AGENT_ALIAS_RE + the 'dashboard.sling' audit type. Dep edge added.
PROGRESS: 

Implementation green.
- shared/src/index.ts: 'dashboard.sling' added to AdminAuditEvent.type union
- backend/src/exec.ts: execGcSling(target, beadText) with AGENT_ALIAS_RE + 4KB cap + 15s timeout
- backend/src/config.ts: maintainerSlingTarget field + parseSlingTarget silent-fallback parser
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: POST /sling handler + DI'd execGcSling option; helpers extracted (composeBeadText, isSlingIntent, isSlingKind)
- backend/src/server.ts: threaded config.maintainerSlingTarget into router options
- backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts: 16 tests covering happy path, all 3 templates, target fallback, target override, 5 validation paths, 2 ExecResult error paths, 3 ExecError paths, stdout-without-bead-id

Tests: 45/45 pass. typecheck + typecheck:test clean.

title: feat(maintainer): triaged-state persistence + per-row toggle + untriaged visual
description:
Foundation for distinguishing items the maintainer has actually vetted from items only mechanically scored by the 7ts heuristic. Today every row shows a triage_score but the page makes no visual distinction between 'this number is your considered judgment' and 'this number is a guess from labels + diff size.' Closes that gap.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- triage_score (introduced in 7ts) is explicitly a HEURISTIC, not a vetted assessment. It combines severity (priority/p* tier) + simplicity (PR diff size, PR status, issue friction labels). It's a starting-point ranking, not 'the maintainer agrees with this priority.'
- Vetted state per item needs its own persistence layer — must survive the nightly worker (ar9) rewriting the cache. Separate JSON file at $HOME/.gascity-dashboard/maintainer-triage-state.json keyed by '<kind>:<number>'. fetchTriage reads it after composeEnvelope and applies triaged + triaged_at to each item.
- Visual signal must pass the Greyscale Test. Editorial answer: italic title + small '?' leading marker (fg-faint) for untriaged; vetted items render at normal weight with no extra marker. NOT a card tint, NOT a side-stripe, NOT a colored dot (the maroon mark already owns that channel for One Mark).

SCOPE
- shared/src/index.ts: add 'triaged: boolean' + 'triaged_at: IsoTimestamp | null' to TriageItem.
- backend/src/maintainer/triaged-state.ts: atomic JSON read/write keyed by '<kind>:<number>'.
- backend/src/maintainer/triage.ts: after composeEnvelope, splice triaged-state onto each item.
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: POST /api/maintainer/triaged with body { items: [{kind, number}], triaged: boolean } — flips one or many items, writes the state file, fires notifyRefresh so any open tab repaints.
- frontend/src/api/client.ts: api.maintainerSetTriaged(items, triaged) wrapper.
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx: italic title + '?' leading marker for triaged=false; per-row 'Mark triaged' affordance (single-click toggle, no confirmation, reversible).

ACCEPTANCE
- Untriaged rows visually distinct in greyscale.
- Mark-triaged action persists across nightly worker refresh (state survives cache rewrite).
- The maroon One Mark continues to apply correctly (the marked item itself can be untriaged — those are independent signals).

title: feat(maintainer): priority badges + triage scores + collapse + breaking-only filter
description: Three polish refinements on top of the closed bead stack: (1) merge bd/beads as topic aliases since they're the same subsystem; (2) surface the labeled severity priority (P0/P1/P2/P3 from priority/p* labels) as a leading badge column on each row, and the computed triage_score in the metadata line; (3) make tier headings + cluster headings click-to-collapse with localStorage persistence, plus a 'Breaking only' top-level filter that hides regression + stability tiers entirely for focused triage sessions.
close_reason: Polish bundle shipped. (1) Topic alias: bd + beads collapse into a single 'beads' canonical topic via the new TopicDef{canonical,patterns[]} structure; mol/molecule similarly merged. (2) PriorityBadge component extracts priority/p[0-3] label from item.labels and renders as small uppercase tracked text-fg-muted in a new leading column on every row; null when unlabeled. triage_score surfaces as 't<n>' in RowMeta after the issue/PR number, fg-faint, with a hover title explaining the formula. (3) useCollapseState hook persists collapsed-id set in localStorage (key maintainer:collapsed); CollapseGlyph rotates ▾ to indicate state. Tier headings and cluster headings are now buttons that toggle. Plus a 'Breaking only' button in the meta slot (persists via maintainer:focusBreaking key) that filters tiers down to regression_breaking for focused triage sessions.

title: feat(maintainer): distinguish issue vs PR rows + cross-link via body parse
description: The 361 ingest left two gaps the page exposes once live data lands: (1) standalone PR rows in unclustered render with pl-6 indent inherited from the 'PR under issue' layout, so an orphan PR reads as a dangling continuation of whatever issue happens to be above it; (2) no PR-to-issue linkage means the 'anchored' affordance never lights up. Fix both: re-add body to GH_PR_FIELDS (fits under MAX_BYTES_LARGE), parse PR bodies for (Fixes|Closes|Resolves) #N to populate pr.linked_numbers, then reverse-map to populate issue.linked_numbers with the PR numbers that fix them. Frontend: give each row a leading typographic kind marker ('PR' small-caps for PRs, blank for issues; maroon mark stays in same column on is_marked items) and stop indenting standalone PRs; only nest PRs when their parent issue is also visible in the current tier section.
close_reason: Two-part fix. Backend: re-added body to GH_PR_FIELDS, parse PR bodies with /\b(?:close[sd]?|fix(?:e[sd])?|resolve[sd]?)\s+#(\d+)\b/gi to populate pr.linked_numbers, then reverse-map to populate issue.linked_numbers with the PRs that fix them. Verified end-to-end: 20 of 143 PRs link to 19 unique open issues. Frontend: extracted RowList component that nests PRs under their parent issue when both are in the same view; introduced four greyscale-safe row states (regular issue / marked issue / standalone PR with 'PR' leading label / nested PR with '↳' glyph + pl-10 indent + muted title). 'anchored' label on issues now only lights up when the linked PR is OUT of view — when it's IN view, the visual nesting already says it.

title: feat(maintainer): priority classifier (labels to tier)
description: Deterministic classifier mapping GitHub labels to triage tier: REGRESSION+BREAKING, REGRESSION, STABILITY. LLM fallback for items without sufficient labels (uses title + body, returns tier). Persists tier on cache row. Cheapest cluster axis and the one carrying the page's primary spatial structure — ships value early because tiered rendering works even before file-overlap and semantic clusters land.
close_reason: Priority classifier + triage scoring ships. Labels -> tier: kind/bug + priority/p0|p1 -> regression_breaking; kind/bug -> regression; everything else -> stability. Triage score combines severity_base (300/200/100 by tier) with a simplicity_bonus: PR diff size + review status for PRs; linked-PR presence + needs-info/needs-repro/stale penalties for issues. composeEnvelope sorts each tier section by score desc so the highest-priority-by-triage-skill items bubble to the top. One Mark Rule enforced: at most one maroon across the entire envelope. Mark candidates are non-blocked PRs in regression_breaking; the top scorer's mark transfers to its parent issue when the parent is in view, so the pair reads as one unit. Verified end-to-end: 47/129/228 tier split on gastownhall/gascity, exactly one mark (issue #2234, fix candidate PR #2462). LLM fallback for unlabeled items deferred — taxonomy coverage is high on this repo; filed as follow-up if needed for other repos.

title: feat(maintainer): gh ingest + maintainer SQLite cache
description: Build the data foundation. gh CLI wrapper that fetches open issues + PRs from gastownhall/gascity, plus a local SQLite cache table for enrichment results (item_id, kind, cluster_id, blast_files, weak_ties, computed_at). Add GET /api/maintainer/triage returning the raw merged list. Includes a manual refresh endpoint for development. No clustering yet; data is flat.
close_reason: Ingest layer ships. gh CLI wrappers (execGhIssueList, execGhPrList) with bumped MAX_BYTES_LARGE for server-controlled JSON payloads; GITHUB_TOKEN passthrough in cleanEnv. Atomic JSON cache at $HOME/.gascity-dashboard/maintainer-cache.json (chose JSON over SQLite: zero new deps, <250KB payload, no multi-process need). GET /api/maintainer/triage reads cache; POST /api/maintainer/refresh writes it. Verified end-to-end: 262 issues + 143 PRs from gastownhall/gascity ingest in ~9s, render correctly in both themes via snap.mjs maintainer. Frontend swap complete: Maintainer.tsx uses useCachedData + api.maintainerTriage(). closingIssuesReferences dropped (not in gh 2.45); PR-issue linkage left for follow-up via body parse or graphql call.

title: feat(maintainer): /maintainer route shell + design tokens
description: Scaffold the new /maintainer route with the editorial-typographic shell — TRIAGE header, date right-aligned, empty tier sections (REGRESSION+BREAKING, REGRESSION, STABILITY), navigation entry. title: Proxy SSE through backend /api/events/stream (decouple from direct supervisor reachability)
description:
Today the frontend opens EventSource directly against gc supervisor URL (http://127.0.0.1:8372/v0/city/{name}/events/stream) per architect addendum td-wisp-ijk7g. This breaks any deployment where the browser can't reach the supervisor's loopback bind — most commonly SSH-tunnel access where only :5174 is forwarded. The Agents page indicator shows 'offline' because EventSource onerror fires immediately.

Fix: pipe SSE through the backend at GET /api/events/stream. Backend opens an upstream connection to the supervisor, forwards Last-Event-ID, and streams events back to the client with the same event name ('event'). Frontend useGcEventRefresh switches to '/api/events/stream'. Drop supervisor URL from CSP connect-src. /api/config/gc-supervisor remains in place but the events hook no longer calls it.

Net effect: only :5174 (dev) or :8081 (prod) need to be reachable from the browser. SSH tunnel works with a single forward. Same-origin SSE also simplifies CSP.
notes:
PLAN: New backend route GET /api/events/stream opens upstream fetch to {supervisorUrl}/v0/city/{name}/events/stream, forwards Last-Event-ID header (or ?after= query) upstream, pipes the raw byte stream verbatim to the client (preserves SSE framing: 'event: event', 'data: ...', 'id: ...'). Mount OUTSIDE writeRouter so no CSRF requirement (EventSource can't carry a CSRF token). Cleanup: client disconnect aborts upstream. Heartbeat: send ':

' every 15s defensively. Frontend useGcEventRefresh drops the /api/config/gc-supervisor fetch and connects EventSource('/api/events/stream'). CSP: drop supervisorUrl from connect-src (now same-origin via 'self'). Files: NEW backend/src/routes/events.ts, NEW backend/test/events.test.ts, MOD backend/src/server.ts, MOD frontend/src/hooks/useGcEvents.ts, MOD backend/src/middleware/security.ts (drop stale comment). Tests: fake upstream SSE server, assert event byte-pipe + Last-Event-ID forward + disconnect cleanup. Risks: timeout middleware on long-lived response — confirm none; intermediaries dropping idle connections — heartbeat defends. Out of scope: removing /api/config/gc-supervisor (will become unused; flag for follow-up).
PLAN-REVIEW: Adversarial check — (a) Does forwarding Last-Event-ID header upstream actually resume correctly? gc supervisor accepts ?after= query per the current FE code; need to map Last-Event-ID header → ?after= upstream OR Last-Event-ID header upstream. Use the same pattern the FE uses today (?after=) for guaranteed parity. (b) Could the byte pipe corrupt SSE framing if a chunk boundary splits a frame? No — SSE is line-delimited and HTTP chunks are arbitrary; both sides parse line-by-line so chunk boundaries don't matter. (c) What if upstream returns non-200 immediately? Surface as a single SSE error event ('event: error
data: {...}

') then close — frontend will see this as a normal message and trigger reconnect via onerror eventually. Cleaner: forward upstream status to client as initial response status; the EventSource will treat non-200 as error. Pick the latter. (d) Does dropping the supervisor URL from CSP break anything else? Grep confirms /api/config/gc-supervisor is the ONLY consumer; safe to drop both the endpoint usage in the FE hook AND the CSP entry.
PROGRESS: implemented backend/src/routes/events.ts + tests (6 cases). Wired into server.ts at /api/events outside writeRouter. Removed /api/config/gc-supervisor endpoint (FE no longer needs it). Frontend useGcEvents now opens EventSource('/api/events/stream'). CSP connect-src reduced to 'self'. Backend 23/23 + frontend 42/42 tests pass. Live e2e against running gc supervisor: SSE headers correct, CSP no longer enumerates supervisor URL.
REVIEW: 3-reviewer Phase 4. All three converged on the SAME HIGH: drain await hangs forever if client disconnects during backpressure (orphan upstream connection + heartbeat timer). FIXED by racing drain against close event with shared resolver, plus a writableEnded check after. Other findings (cast removal in test, trailing-slash doc on supervisorUrl, redundant try/catch around cancel().catch(), heartbeat rationale comment) all addressed inline. Skipped: backpressure test (would need a fake slow client + chunked upstream; lower ROI than implementation polish). MEDIUM (no upstream fetch header timeout) deferred — loopback risk is small.
close_reason: DONE: SSE proxy live at GET /api/events/stream. Backend opens upstream fetch to gc supervisor, byte-pipes the SSE stream, supports Last-Event-ID/?after= resume, aborts upstream on client disconnect with drain-race protection. /api/config/gc-supervisor removed. CSP connect-src now 'self'. 23/23 backend + 42/42 frontend tests pass. Merged on main at edec592. Operator action: hard-reload the dashboard in the browser to pick up the new useGcEvents.ts; only :5174 (or :8081 in prod) now needs to be reachable from the browser.

title: Stale-while-revalidate cache for cross-route page swaps
description: Each route (Agents, Beads, Mail, Activity, Health) does useState(empty) + useEffect(() => api.listX()) on mount, so navigating away and back is always a cold fetch — currently ~1.4s per supervisor call. Add a module-level Map cache in frontend/src/api/cache.ts and a useCachedFetch(key, fetcher) hook that (a) renders cached data immediately on mount if present, (b) always kicks off a background refresh, (c) updates state when fresh data arrives, (d) keeps showing cached data on fetch error. Refactor each route to use the hook. Cache key per endpoint+params (e.g. agents, beads:open, mail:inbox:stephanie). Mutations call cache.invalidate(prefix) so the next render fetches fresh. Result: first visit is normal speed; subsequent swaps are instant + refresh in background.
close_reason: DONE: Added frontend/src/api/cache.ts (module-level Map with get/set/invalidate/invalidateKey) and frontend/src/hooks/useCachedData.ts (stale-while-revalidate hook). Refactored Agents, Beads, Mail, Activity, Health to use it — second visit to any route renders cached data synchronously while a background fetch refreshes. Refresh-button + SSE refreshes still flow through hook.refresh() and rewrite the cache. Mutations in-route auto-refresh via existing refresh() calls; cache.invalidate(prefix) available if future cross-route writes need it.

title: CSRF self-heal: frontend retries once on 403 kind=csrf
description: Each backend boot generates a new in-process CSRF bootToken (backend/src/middleware/csrf.ts). tsx watch restarts the backend on every source-file edit, rotating the token; the browser still holds the previous cookie until the next GET returns Set-Cookie. Mutations (peek, mail send, bead actions) made in that window 403 with kind:csrf. Fix in frontend/src/api/client.ts: when a non-GET request returns 403 with kind:csrf, fetch GET /api/csrf to refresh the cookie, then replay the original request exactly once. Single retry, no recursion, no retry on any other error class.
close_reason: DONE: api/client.ts wraps request() and self-heals on 403 kind=csrf by fetching /api/csrf to refresh the cookie, then replaying the original request once. Single retry, no recursion. Verified semantics by reading csrf middleware — token rotates per bootToken (per process), so frontend retry after the cookie-refresh GET sees the new value.

title: Group + filter + search across Beads, Agents, Mail views
description: The three list-heavy views (Beads, Agents, Mail) all surface items from multiple projects mixed together, with no way to focus on one project at a time and no in-page search. As the queue grows this is the main friction point. Design: (1) collapse/expand by project — each project gets a section header with a count badge and a chevron disclosure; remembered per-view per-session in localStorage. (2) search — a single text input per view that filters the visible list (substring match across title + identifier + relevant fields like 'from' for mail, 'session id' for agents). (3) filter chips for the high-signal axes: status for beads (open/in_progress/blocked/closed), state for agents (running/idle/stopped), read-state for mail (unread/read). Respect existing per-view conventions: beads already groups by status implicitly via the table; agents shows session state via the row; mail uses the persistent 'Reading as' strip. Don't add color or new components if existing utility classes can handle it (Greyscale Test). Out of scope: full-text search across mail bodies (defer to a separate bead). Acceptance: on a screen showing 30+ items, the operator can find a specific item in under 5 seconds without keyboard navigation.

title: Context % wildly wrong for mayor session (shows 75%, tmux shows 15%)
description: The Agents view shows context usage of ~75% for the mayor session, but the actual live tmux session is fresh and reports ~15%. Five-fold overestimate. Likely a parsing or sampling bug in how the dashboard derives context % from the supervisor's session state. Investigate: (1) backend/src/gc-client.ts — how is context % extracted from the gc CLI response? Is the value a percent already, or a ratio, or a token count that needs normalization? (2) frontend/src/routes/agents.* — is the display layer doing any of its own math on the value? (3) shared/src/types — what's the wire-shape contract? Compare against what tmux/mayor itself reports for the same session at the same moment. May be a unit mismatch (tokens vs %), a stale-cache issue, or a wrong divisor. Acceptance: context % in the dashboard matches what mayor/tmux reports for the same session, within rounding.

title: Investigate slow /api/sessions and /api/beads endpoints
description: Both endpoints intermittently time out (>10s). Surfaces as 'No sessions running' / 'Nothing on the queue right now' empty states in the dashboard even though the gc supervisor has real data. Likely a slow shell-out to gc CLI in the backend's gc-client.ts. Profile backend/src/routes/sessions.ts and backend/src/routes/beads.ts to find the hot path; consider caching, parallel fetch, or talking to the supervisor's HTTP API directly instead of shelling out.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128 on branch=worktree-agent-aa01f312a2a328f6e contains base=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128
PLAN: Root cause is NOT shell-out — gc-client.ts already uses HTTP fetch. Real issue: GcClient.getJson() has NO default timeout. Node 20 fetch has no default timeout either. When the gc supervisor hangs or is slow on /sessions or /beads (beads is 1000-item window, sessions can be large), the backend request hangs indefinitely until the browser bails (>10s). Routes never pass an AbortSignal so they are effectively unbounded.

Approach: (1) Add default per-request timeout in GcClient.getJson via AbortSignal.timeout, configurable per call (default 5000ms — short enough to fail fast, long enough for the 1000-bead window). (2) Pile on a small in-memory TTL cache wrapper (e.g. 2s) for listSessions/listBeads so simultaneous tab loads / SSE refreshes collapse to one upstream call (in-flight de-dup). (3) Convert 502 hang into a 504-with-message so the empty-state path is reached quickly.

Files: backend/src/gc-client.ts (add timeout + accept default), backend/src/routes/sessions.ts (translate AbortError -> 504), backend/src/routes/beads.ts (same), backend/src/cache.ts (new — TTL+inflight memo, small). Tests: backend/test/gc-client.test.ts (timeout fires), backend/test/cache.test.ts (TTL + inflight dedup).

Risks: (a) 5s ceiling on listBeads(limit=1000) might be too tight when supervisor is under load — bump if profiling shows it. (b) Caching reads of dynamic state means an action-then-immediate-reload may see stale; 2s window is short enough that the SSE event-driven refresh still feels live. (c) Adding a tiny test runner increases dev-dep surface.

Out of scope: frontend changes, mail/git/builds/health, ring-buffer source, anything not on /api/sessions or /api/beads hot path.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) Use Node 20 built-in node:test runner — zero new deps. (b) Drop TTL from MVP; in-flight request coalescing (single-flight per URL) is the higher-value, lower-staleness simplification. Two simultaneous /api/sessions calls collapse to one upstream call. (c) Translate AbortError -> 504 'upstream timeout' so the user sees a fast empty state, not a 10s+ hang. (d) Keep default timeout configurable via env (DEFAULT_GC_TIMEOUT_MS, default 5000) so it can be tuned without code change. (e) Minimum diff: gc-client.ts (timeout + coalescing wrapper), routes/sessions.ts (504 mapping), routes/beads.ts (504 mapping), plus tests in backend/test/. No route signature changes.
PROGRESS: Implemented (1) default 5s timeout in GcClient via AbortSignal.timeout (configurable via GC_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS env or opts.defaultTimeoutMs); (2) in-flight request coalescing — concurrent identical GETs share a single upstream call; per-caller signal is composed at await-layer so one caller's abort doesn't kill a coalesced fetch; (3) GcClient.isTimeoutError static for clean error classification; (4) sessions.ts + beads.ts map timeout -> 504 'upstream-timeout', non-timeout failure stays 502 'upstream'. Tests: 11 passing in backend/test/{gc-client,routes}.test.ts using node:test (no new deps) + tsx via 'npm run test'. Both typecheck targets pass.

Frontend typecheck has pre-existing baseline failures (vitest not installed, missing shared export effectiveContextPct) — verified out of scope by stash-and-recheck.
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran in parallel. code-reviewer + ts-reviewer both flagged isTimeoutError over-broad (AbortError arm conflated caller-abort with timeout). Fixed in-wave: narrowed to TimeoutError only + updated caller-signal test to positively assert AbortError + !isTimeoutError. Filed gascity-dashboard-mek (extend 504 mapping to mail.ts/health.ts) and gascity-dashboard-ais (strip upstream URL from fetchOnce error). 11 backend tests + both backend typechecks green on merged review branch.
close_reason: DONE: GcClient hot-path bounded. (1) Default per-request timeout via AbortSignal.timeout (env GC_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS, default 5000ms). (2) In-flight request coalescing per URL — concurrent identical GETs share one upstream call; caller signals compose at await layer so one abort doesn't kill the shared fetch. (3) Routes map timeout to HTTP 504 upstream-timeout; non-timeout failures stay 502. Hypothesis in description (slow shell-out) was wrong — gc-client already used HTTP; root cause was no timeout. 11 node:test tests, no new deps. Phase 4 narrowed isTimeoutError to TimeoutError only. Merged via wave-kz8-9yb-review at 15ded785e58857f3ce166e2eefd84ab6c7040cf0.

title: refactor(backend): DI audit log path via recordAudit param instead of module-level setAuditLogPath
description:
Phase 4 review of bead ib5 (ts-reviewer MEDIUM #7) flagged that audit.ts uses a module-level 'let logPath' mutated by setAuditLogPath(). This forces tests to run with { concurrency: false } and risks cross-file races if a second backend test ever calls setAuditLogPath() concurrently.

The DI pattern used for execGcSling in ib5 (router options carry the function) is the precedent. The natural fix:
- Replace module-level logPath with a per-call argument or a factory: makeAuditRecorder(logPath) returns recordAudit
- Server constructs the recorder once at startup with config.auditLogPath and threads it into routers that need it
- Tests construct their own recorder with a tmp path, no global mutation

Scope:
- backend/src/audit.ts: refactor to factory + remove setAuditLogPath
- All callers of recordAudit (server.ts wiring, every route): thread the recorder
- Tests: remove setAuditLogPath usage and remove { concurrency: false } guards
- Optional cleanup follow-up: also retrofit execMailSend / execAgentNudge to be DI-able for the same reasons

Out of scope of ib5; non-blocking for ib5 since the test file uses concurrency:false correctly.

title: feat(maintainer): agent-vetted triage assessment overrides heuristic score
description:
The endgame for the triage_score: today it's a label-driven heuristic, but once an item has been routed to the triage agent (bead 4) and the agent has done its assessment, that assessment should override the heuristic score visibly. Closes the loop on the maintainer's question 'are the triage values from our triage skill assessment right?' — for vetted items, yes; for the rest, no, they're heuristics, and the page should make the difference legible.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- Agent return path is async + indirect. The triage agent (chief-of-staff routing to project lead) does its work in its own bead/wisp; the dashboard sees the outcome via the next gh refresh — typically through one of:
    - A GitHub comment on the item with a structured assessment (parse via JSON-fenced code block convention)
    - A label change ('triage/vetted-p0', 'triage/vetted-p2-stable', etc.)
    - A linked PR or comment by the operator's gh user
  Open question to resolve in implementation: which signal is canonical? Likely a label convention is cleanest (e.g., 'triage/vetted' + 'triage/severity-<n>' + 'triage/simplicity-<band>') because labels are structured and machine-parseable. Decide with the maintainer at start of session.
- Wire shape: add 'triage_assessment: { vetted_score: number, source: 'agent' | 'manual', notes: string, vetted_at: IsoTimestamp } | null' to TriageItem. Null means heuristic-only (the t-prefix score is the only thing we have).
- Visual rule: when triage_assessment is present, render the score in normal text-fg weight with a small ✓ glyph (or just no prefix). When null, render 't<n>' in fg-faint italic as it does today — communicates 'this number is a guess.'
- Manual ack via 'Mark triaged' (bead 4) is NOT an assessment — it sets triaged=true but leaves triage_assessment=null. The maintainer is saying 'I've eyeballed this' not 'the score is correct.' If the maintainer wants to override the heuristic manually, that needs its own UI (edit-in-place on the score), deferred.

SCOPE
- backend/src/maintainer/triage-assessment.ts: parser that reads gh labels (or comments) per item and extracts vetted_score + source + notes.
- shared/src/index.ts: TriageAssessment type + triage_assessment field on TriageItem.
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx: RowMeta renders triage_score with the vetted vs heuristic visual distinction.
- Item sort within tier uses vetted_score when present, falls back to triage_score otherwise.

ACCEPTANCE
- Items with a triage_assessment render scores in normal weight + check glyph.
- Items without render in fg-faint italic with the 't' prefix (current behavior).
- Vetted items sort correctly within their tier using vetted_score.
- The convention for how agent output is signalled (label vs comment) is decided + documented in this bead's notes before implementation lands.

DEPENDS ON: triaged-state (bead 1), sling backend (bead 2), bulk actions (bead 4).

title: feat(maintainer): bulk-select untriaged + 'Mark triaged' / 'Send to triage agent' actions
description:
Adds a selection mode for batch triage on untriaged items. Two buttons, side-by-side per the maintainer's stated preference: a manual ack ('Mark triaged' — flips state without dispatching anyone) and an agent-dispatched assessment ('Send to triage agent' — slings the batch to a triage agent and marks them triaged).

DESIGN CONTEXT
- Checkboxes only appear on untriaged rows; triaged rows have no checkbox (already-vetted, nothing to bulk-act on).
- Selection state lives in MaintainerPage component, not persisted — refresh clears the selection. That's the right scope: bulk triage is a 'do it now' operation, not a saved view.
- A bottom-pinned action bar appears when ≥1 untriaged row is selected. Reads: 'N selected · [Mark triaged] [Send to triage agent] [Clear]'. The bar disappears when selection is empty. Editorial: NOT a sticky bottom toolbar with chrome — a single typeset line at the bottom of the page that pins to the viewport bottom only when selection > 0.
- 'Mark triaged': POSTs to /api/maintainer/triaged with the batch, marks all in one call.
- 'Send to triage agent': POSTs to /api/maintainer/sling with intent='triage' for each selected item (or a single sling with a list-of-URLs text bead — TBD in implementation). After successful sling, marks all selected as triaged. The agent's actual assessment arrives async via the next refresh.
- Target for the triage agent: per the maintainer's stated preference, 'maybe optimally this would be a ping to the chief of staff to route for us to the project lead.' v1 default for intent='triage' should be 'chief-of-staff' (with env override MAINTAINER_TRIAGE_TARGET). The sling exec wrapper from bead 2 doesn't care about target identity beyond format validation.

SCOPE
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx:
    - Checkbox column added to untriaged rows (additional leading grid column for untriaged only — 1.5em).
    - Selection state in MaintainerPage; passed down via prop.
    - 'Triage actions' bottom bar (renders only when selection.size > 0).
    - Click 'Mark triaged': batch call to maintainerSetTriaged.
    - Click 'Send to triage agent': fan out N sling calls in parallel (limit concurrency 5), then batch maintainerSetTriaged.
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: confirm POST /sling tolerates intent='triage' (added in bead 2's template map).

ACCEPTANCE
- Checkboxes visible on untriaged rows only.
- Bottom action bar appears/disappears with selection size.
- 'Mark triaged' on N selected updates all N rows + persists.
- 'Send to triage agent' dispatches all + marks triaged + audit-logs each sling.
- Failures on individual slings surface inline (don't block the batch).

title: feat(maintainer): per-row sling buttons for vetted items (review PR / draft from issue)
description:
Adds an inline 'sling' affordance to every triaged row: 'Review' for PRs, 'Draft' for issues. Only triaged items get the button — the maintainer's stated rule is 'as long as we have already vetted the ones that show up at the top.' Untriaged items have to be marked triaged (either manually or by the triage-agent batch) before they're eligible.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- The button lives in RowMeta or as a final inline element after the status word. Editorial: NOT a button-with-chrome — a small text link 'sling →' in fg-muted with hover state. Click fires a same-origin POST and the row optimistically updates to 'slung 0m ago' until the next refresh confirms.
- Per-row default target is mayor (per the triaged-state bead's env). A subtle dropdown caret next to 'sling' could let the maintainer pick a different agent (chief-of-staff, project-lead) per-click; defer that to a follow-up — v1 ships single default + env override.
- Intent maps:
    item.kind == 'pr'    → intent='review'
    item.kind == 'issue' → intent='draft'
- After slung, the response includes a bead_id (from gc sling stdout); store it on the local row state so the affordance becomes a link 'slung as <bead_id>' for the rest of the session. Doesn't need to persist across reloads (bead 5 covers the async return path).

SCOPE
- frontend/src/api/client.ts: api.maintainerSling({ kind, number, html_url, intent, target? })
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx:
    - RowMeta gets an optional sling action element when item.triaged === true.
    - On click: optimistic UI ('slinging…'), POST, update to 'slung Xm ago' on success or error message on failure.
    - Same-session in-memory map of {kind:number} → { bead_id, slung_at } so the affordance is restoration-stable for the operator's working session.

ACCEPTANCE
- Untriaged rows show no sling affordance.
- Triaged rows show 'review →' (PR) or 'draft →' (issue).
- Click successfully routes to mayor and records audit. Failure surfaces inline without console errors.
- Greyscale Test: the sling affordance reads in greyscale (it's just text + arrow glyph).

title: feat(maintainer): live SSE status overlay
description: Status, age, assignment refresh live over the cached enrichment. Reuses the existing SSE pattern from Activity. Only the volatile fields (status word, age, assignee) refresh; cluster membership and trust tier stay anchored to the nightly snapshot. Respects prefers-reduced-motion: changes fade in over 150ms, no slide. Closes the loop between ambient-glance freshness and triage-investigation depth.
close_reason: SSE 'refreshed' notification ships. GET /api/maintainer/events keeps a long-lived text/event-stream open per client; whenever the cache is rewritten (manual button or nightly worker), notifyRefresh pushes an 'event: refreshed' with the new computed_at + repo, and every open frontend tab refetches /triage. csrfValidate exempts GET so SSE lives in the same writeRouter as the rest of /api/maintainer. Tiny in-process Set<Response> pub/sub in backend/src/maintainer/sse.ts; on client disconnect or write failure the response is dropped. Frontend opens EventSource in useEffect on mount, listens for 'refreshed', calls refresh(); EventSource auto-reconnects on its own. End-to-end verified: subscribe -> POST /refresh -> event arrives within 100ms.

title: feat(maintainer): nightly enrichment worker
description: Cron-like worker that runs the expensive enrichment passes on a schedule (blast-radius nightly, contributor stats nightly, semantic clusters weekly). Idempotent: writes to maintainer.sqlite, marks computed_at. Footer on the /maintainer page reads freshness from the latest computed_at and displays 'clusters computed 2026-05-21 03:00 · 19h ago'. No external scheduler; runs in-process inside the backend with a simple time check on tick.
close_reason: Nightly enrichment worker ships. In-process setInterval scheduler in backend/src/maintainer/worker.ts; runs fetchTriage + writeCache + notifyRefresh once at boot (after 5s delay) then on the configured cadence. Errors log + continue, never crash the backend. Defaults: 6h interval (was going to be 24h but 6h is more 'live feel' without being chatty); 0 disables for manual-refresh-only ops. Heartbeat every 30s keeps open SSE connections alive through proxies/idle-timeouts. Wired into server.ts main() alongside startDoltNomsSampler. Verified at startup: cache mtime updated within seconds of backend boot.

title: feat(maintainer): semantic weak-ties via embeddings
description: Cross-cluster similarity surface. Embedding model over title + body, agglomerative cluster to find groups that share a topic without sharing files. Surfaces as 'also in: <topic> (n)' right-aligned secondary type on rows whose primary cluster is file-overlap. Most expensive enrichment, ships last because the page reads correctly without it. Recompute weekly rather than nightly to bound cost.
close_reason: Deterministic topic-keyword clustering ships (deviation from original embeddings spec — documented). Repo subsystems (session, agent, pool, bd, dolt, pack, mayor, supervisor, etc.) hit hard in gastownhall/gascity titles; a dictionary regex match groups items by subsystem at zero LLM cost. Runs as second clustering pass after file-overlap: file clusters claim authoritative members first, topic-keyword picks up the residue. Cluster coverage jumped from 27 -> 58 clusters; unclustered dropped from 320 -> 109. Frontend renders topic clusters with leading interpunct glyph + uppercase tracked label (e.g. '· SESSION') vs file clusters' path-style header — visual distinction without forking the cluster wire type. weak_ties on TriageItem remains [] — the 'also in: <topic>' multi-membership signal from the original spec wasn't implemented; ship that as a follow-up if the maintainer ever needs cross-cluster annotations. The embedding model approach itself stays a future option for repos where the dictionary doesn't fit.

title: feat(maintainer): contributor stats + trust tier
description: Per-login reputation: issues opened vs accepted (turned into PRs or labeled accepted), PRs opened vs merged. Computed via gh api search/issues per contributor, cached 24h. Maps to trust tier word: core / trusted / regular / new / spam-risk. Tier shown inline always (greyscale-readable); ratios revealed on hover as inline byline expansion (no popover chrome). GET /api/maintainer/contributor/:login for the detail panel.
close_reason: Real contributor trust tiers + merge ratios ship. Bulk-fetch approach: one gh issue list --state all + one gh pr list --state all (two extra parallel calls in fetchTriage) covers the full lifetime history once. Per-author tallies derived in memory; no per-login round-trips that would hit gh's 30 req/min search rate limit. Tier mapping: prs_merged >= 20 -> core; >= 5 OR issues_accepted >= 10 -> trusted; >= 5 contributions + 0 accepted + 0 merged -> spam_risk; >= 1 accepted/merged -> regular; <= 1 total -> new. GET /api/maintainer/contributor/:login reads the stat off the existing envelope (single source of truth, no separate cache). Verified end-to-end on gastownhall/gascity: 86 core / 117 trusted / 169 regular / 32 new / 0 spam_risk across 404 visible rows; sjarmak at 102/118 PRs reads as core, mattiasw at 1/2 reads as regular. Known limitation: gh 2.45 doesn't expose stateReason on  so all CLOSED issues count as accepted (not just COMPLETED); not_planned closures inflate accept rates slightly but don't move the tier needle in this repo. Refresh now takes ~13s (was ~10s).

title: feat(maintainer): blast-radius enrichment for issues + PRs
description: Compute the file-overlap cluster axis. For PRs: derive touched files from git diff (cheap, deterministic). For issues: LLM extraction from title + body to predict likely-touched files, with confidence score; low-confidence issues land in UNCLUSTERED. Persist blast_files and a cluster_id derived from file-set intersection. Optional sourcegraph MCP enrichment for cross-repo references where useful. This is the signal that lets the maintainer batch related work.
close_reason: File-overlap clustering ships. PRs already carry blast_files from gh's existing file metadata (added 'files' to GH_PR_FIELDS — adds ~150KB to the payload, well within MAX_BYTES_LARGE). Issues inherit blast_files from any linked open PR via inheritIssueFiles so an issue and its in-view fix candidate cluster together. buildClusters: greedy by file co-occurrence — for each file, the set of items touching it forms a candidate cluster; biggest groups claim their members first; singletons fall to unclustered. cluster.files surfaces the files appearing in >=50% of cluster members (max 3), giving 'central concern' rather than every incidental touched file. Verified end-to-end on gastownhall/gascity: 1 cluster in regression_breaking, 8 in regression, 18 in stability — biggest is a 21-item docs/schema/city-schema.json + internal/config/config.go cluster with 56102 lines pending. Issue blast-radius LLM extraction (for issues w/o linked PRs) deferred to follow-up — gh-only path covers the maintainer's most-actionable subset (active work) cleanly.

title: Kanban: column-transition activity feed (cd-6w92 port)
description: Port upstream cd-6w92 (Wldc4rd/citadel): client-side diff between Kanban refreshes detects column transitions and renders a rolling 'recent moves' feed below the board, plus a transient ring-highlight on moved cards. Useful for the 'I blinked, what just changed?' moment. Deferred from gascity-dashboard-dh6 v0. Visual treatment will need DESIGN.md re-skin (no card chrome).
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=edec5929959cd371c33d7758d60e6572a6987525 on branch=worktree-agent-ac8f2937e23464140 contains base=edec5929959cd371c33d7758d60e6572a6987525
PLAN:
Goal: Port cd-6w92 spirit — detect bead column transitions across Kanban refreshes, render a 'Recent moves' typographic feed below the board, and apply a brief ring-highlight on moved cards.

Approach:
- Pure detector: detectMoves(prev: KanbanResponse | null, next: KanbanResponse, atMs: number): Move[]. A move = { id, title, from, to, at }. Skip first-snapshot (prev=null) to avoid initial-load noise.
- State: useRef holds last snapshot. useState holds capped move log. useEffect compares each render's data vs ref, appends new moves, evicts old (>2 min) or beyond cap (12).
- Render: 'Recent moves' is a section below the board, no card chrome — uppercase tracked label head, divide-y rows, tabular-figure relative time, e.g. 'gascity-dashboard-dta · in_flight → in_review · 12s'. No saturated colors. Greyscale-safe.
- Ring-highlight: pass recentlyMovedIds set down to Column → Card. Card gets a className 'ring-flash' on member match; CSS keyframe fades a 1px ring of fg-faint over 1.5s. Highlight membership is time-bound (lives ~1.8s after the move), tracked in same hook.
- Feature is purely client-side ephemeral. No server persistence.

Files to touch:
- frontend/src/hooks/kanbanMoves.ts (new) — pure detectMoves + useKanbanMoves hook
- frontend/src/hooks/kanbanMoves.test.ts (new) — detectMoves unit tests
- frontend/src/routes/Kanban.tsx — wire the hook, render RecentMoves section, pass recentIds to cards
- frontend/src/styles/index.css — @keyframes ring-flash + .ring-flash utility

Tests:
- detectMoves: null prev returns []; same snapshot returns []; bead moving from col A to col B yields one Move; bead disappearing from one col (closed_24h eviction) does not synthesize a phantom move if not present in any new column (i.e. treat 'no longer on the board' as not a move); bead in same column with different title is not a move; multiple moves in one diff.

Risks:
- False positives on initial load: handled by ignoring prev=null.
- Unbounded feed: handled by N=12 cap + 2 min TTL.
- Ring-highlight flicker on rapid re-renders: keyed via uniqueMoveId, so a single move emits a single ring.

Out of scope: server persistence, drag/drop, snap diff (main session does it later).
PLAN-REVIEW:
1. Cap+TTL math: with 12 cap and 2 min TTL, a rapid burst of >12 moves loses early ones. Fine — the feed is 'recent', not 'audit log'. Acceptable.
2. Ring-highlight 'recentlyMovedIds' membership eviction: must use a setTimeout, not just on next refresh, or the ring sticks for 30s until next refresh tick. Will track with per-move timer.
3. Same bead moving twice: detector keys current state on bead-id; if A→B→C between two snapshots, we only see A→C (best we can do client-side). Acceptable.
4. Bead leaving the board (closed_24h eviction after >24h): old code would never see it in any new column. detectMoves must NOT synthesize a 'A → gone' move. Filter: only emit if next has the bead.
5. Title can be missing/changed; use new snapshot's title in the Move record.
6. Re-rendering issue: appending to state inside useEffect that depends on data — fine, but must guard against the prev ref pointing to a snapshot we never wrote new moves for. Set prevRef.current = data at the end of effect regardless.
7. Visual: 'A → B' arrow uses a real → glyph (not em-dash). DESIGN.md bans em dash but → is fine.
8. Accessibility: prefers-reduced-motion already nullifies animations globally via styles/index.css. Good.
9. Tests must run pure — no React. Just import detectMoves and call with fixtures.
PROGRESS: detectMoves pure function + useKanbanMoves hook landed with 10 unit tests (all GREEN). Kanban.tsx wired: passes recentMoveIds down to Card for ring-flash class; renders RecentMoves section below board. CSS keyframe added for the 1.5s warm-amber ring fade. Full frontend tests pass (52/52), typecheck clean, vite build clean.

Code-reviewer confirmed DESIGN.md compliance: → glyph, no card chrome, warm-amber neutral ring (not saturated), prefers-reduced-motion respected, tabular figures.

title: Strip upstream URL from gc-client fetchOnce error message
description: Pre-existing finding from wave-3 security review. backend/src/gc-client.ts fetchOnce throws a string like "gc supervisor returned <status> for <url>", and routes (sessions.ts, beads.ts, mail.ts) forward err.message verbatim into the 502 response body details.message. The url includes the supervisor port and city name (http://127.0.0.1:8372/v0/city/<cityName>/...), visible in browser dev tools. Backend is loopback-only so blast radius is small, but no reason to leak topology. Fix: drop the URL from the thrown error; the route-level kind:upstream discriminant is enough for the frontend.

title: Mobile and tablet responsive design
description: Per PRODUCT.md the target is MacBook Pro 16-inch primary plus occasional host console, so phone is not in scope. But the Header + Tables collapse fragile below ~960px. Decide if a basic tablet/phone layout is worth adding for emergency-debug-from-couch use, and if so, design the breakpoints (probably: collapse nav to a Menu disclosure under 768px, allow horizontal-scroll on tables, drop max-width to 100vw with side padding).
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-afe89fed768ae2234 contains base
PLAN: Option B chosen. PRODUCT.md L9-12 specifies 'MacBook Pro... occasional host console directly' as the entire device surface. Anti-references reject density/feature-creep ('every dashboard tries to show everything'). No mention of phone, tablet, or remote-couch use. The bead's couch use case is speculative. SCOPE: stabilize 768-1280px (the fragile range that occurs naturally when the SSH-forwarded port is opened in a smaller browser window on the MacBook). APPROACH: (1) Layout/Header: reduce horizontal padding from px-8 to px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, reduce inter-nav gap (gap-7 -> gap-x-5 sm:gap-x-7) so the header row stops fighting at narrow widths; (2) Header inner: allow flex-wrap on the second row of bursts (already mostly flex). Keep header on a single row down to ~720px (5 nav + brand + toggle). Allow wrap only at narrower widths; (3) Tables: keep existing overflow-x-auto, no changes needed. FILES: frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx, frontend/src/components/Header.tsx, README.md (note device support). TESTS: typecheck only (no test suite for visual components in this repo). RISKS: changing padding affects all 5 routes; rhythm must remain. OUT OF SCOPE: hamburger nav, phone layout, horizontal-scroll shadow indicators, drawer mail layout.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Padding change is global and affects every route — verified by reviewing PageHeader, Layout, all 5 routes; routes don't set their own outer padding, they sit inside Layout's main. Safe. (2) Header gap reduction must not change vertical rhythm — gap-x only, not gap-y. (3) Header could go single-row down to ~720px with px-4 + gap-x-5; below that the operator is in territory we are explicitly not supporting. We will NOT add flex-wrap to the nav; the bead description proposed it but per Option B we don't redesign sub-720, we just stop the fragile collapse in the supported range. (4) DESIGN.md compliance: no new colors, no cards, no second typeface, no em-dashes. The change is purely spacing tokens. (5) Greyscale Test: spacing has no color; untouched. (6) One Mark Rule: untouched. (7) Tabular figures: untouched (Table component already applies tnum). (8) README note: brief, factual, no scope expansion. No risks to design contract.
PROGRESS: (Phase 2) Layout main padding: px-8 -> px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8. Header outer: same padding + gap-x-6 lg:gap-x-8 gap-y-2 flex-wrap (was gap-8 no wrap). Header nav ul: gap-x-5 lg:gap-x-7 gap-y-1 flex-wrap (was gap-7 no wrap). README: added 'Supported device surface' paragraph after Quick start citing PRODUCT.md, layout stable from ~720px upward, phone explicitly out of scope. (Phase 3) Diff reviewed; minimal — 3 files of code + README. Typecheck clean (frontend). No DESIGN.md regressions: no new colors, no cards, no second typeface, no em-dashes in UI, .tnum on tables untouched.

title: kanbanMoves indexById: first-wins guard for duplicate ids
description: Phase 4 ts-reviewer LOW: frontend/src/hooks/kanbanMoves.ts indexById() silently overwrites if the gc supervisor ever emits a snapshot where the same bead id appears in two columns (malformed data). Affected: a bead could disappear from detectMoves or get a phantom 'from' column depending on map-insertion order. Fix: 'if (!map.has(c.id))' first-wins, or a dev-only assertion to surface the invariant. Low severity because the gc classifier is expected to guarantee each bead appears in exactly one column.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=9492e52fd69fd5efcce93e0cf8cdfedba9ed99dd on branch=worktree-agent-a09471ea200a12023 contains base=9492e52fd69fd5efcce93e0cf8cdfedba9ed99dd
PLAN: (1) Add first-wins guard in indexById — wrap map.set with !map.has(c.id). (2) Add 2 tests in kanbanMoves.test.ts covering duplicate id in next-snapshot and prev-snapshot, asserting the first column wins. Tests written first (RED), then one-line fix (GREEN).
PLAN-REVIEW: First-wins is the better choice over an assertion — the invariant is supervisor-side, and a thrown error here would break the entire kanban view for a malformed snapshot. Tolerant detection is consistent with how detectMoves already handles missing prev/next entries by simply skipping. Edge case: an identical-id placed in two different prev columns and one of those same columns in next could in worst case produce a phantom no-op move; first-wins makes the behavior deterministic and the test will pin it.

Worktree learning: harness 'isolation: worktree' subagents inherit cwd state in surprising ways. After a subagent worktree gets removed by some hook, attempts to create a new orchestrator worktree via relative paths can land at deeply nested locations under a former subagent dir.
